sglang.title: Use device_id in dist init to reduce NCCL communicator warmup & creation overhead
sglang.author: Edenzzzz
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
As explained by the PyTorch team (pytorch/pytorch#129140, pytorch/pytorch#129147), NCCL communicators associated with a process group is initialized lazily when a primitive is called. You can see this in init_process_group doc string.
Setting a device_id eagerly inits it. It should reduce

sglang.title: [fix] fix potential bumpy throughtput with deepgemm
sglang.author: Alcanderian
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
DeepGEMM load kernel symbol at the first time it called, so we better to load it the at the launch stage on all rank to avoid bumpy throughput at the serving stage.
Another purpose: Let all ranks compile/load kernel at the same time, avoiding DeepEP commuication time out.
See  def __call_

Motivation
When running compile_deep_gemm.py in a multi-node scenario, follower nodes (Rank > 0) encounter a `404 Not Found  error.  This PR fixes the issue.

sglang.title: [feature] support for roberta embedding models
sglang.author: DavidBao03
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This PR aims to add support for Roberta-based embedding models (such as BGE-M3) in SGLang, which currently lacks native support for these architectures. The changes will enable users to leverage Roberta-based models for their embedding needs within the SGLang framework.

sglang.title: Handle JSONDecodeError while processing request data
sglang.author: yan97ao
sglang.bodyExcerpt: When parsing incoming request bodies, a malformed JSON payload would raise an uncaught JSONDecodeError, causing a 500 response. This change catches JSONDecodeError and returns a 400 Bad Request with a descriptive error message.

vllm.title: [V1] Add `structural_tag` support using xgrammar
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This change introduces support for a new structured output format
introduced in Xgrammar. It allows specifying a json schema for
structured output that occurs in between a beginning and end tag within
a response.
This PR is intended as a first step toward making use of this
functionality. There is m

vllm.title: [BUGFIX] use random for NONE_HASH only when PYTHONHASHSEED not set
vllm.author: andyxning
vllm.bodyExcerpt: According to the comments, NONE_HASH can only use random bytes when PYTHONHASHSEED is not set. For now, the implementation is in the opposite.

vllm.title: [Chore] added stubs for `vllm_flash_attn` during development mode
vllm.author: aarnphm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
With #17159, whenever vLLM is installed with VLLM_USE_PRECOMPILED,
it will inadvertently copy the __init__.py from vllm_flash_attn, which will generate bad diff
This PR removes the __init__.py copy in setup.py, and add a function stubs for all functions within the flash_attn_interface.py

the backends available are different
request-level backend choice is no longer su

vllm.title: [Bugfix] fix error due to an uninitialized tokenizer when using `skip_tokenizer_init` with `num_scheduler_steps`
vllm.author: junstar92
vllm.bodyExcerpt: When using skip_tokenizer_init with num_scheduler_steps (scheduling multiple steps), an error occurs due to an uninitialized tokenizer. This PR adds a condition to check tokenizer initialization as single_step.py did.

vllm.title: [Kernel][Triton][FP8] Adding fp8 and variable length sequence support to Triton FAv2 kernel
vllm.author: rasmith
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds fp8 and variable length sequence support to Triton FAv2 kernel.
This kernel supports 8-bit KV cache, and also the following (forward only):

Fwd with causal masking
Arbitrary Q and KV sequence lengths
Arbitrary head sizes
Multi and grouped query attention
Variable sequence lengths
ALiBi

vllm.title: [Misc] Make cached tokenizer pickle-compatible
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This is required to support running multi-modal processor in parallel using multi-processing (which is WIP).
I also changed all_special_tokens and similar methods to return a list instead of a set, since that's how it is in the HF-defined classes. Not sure why they were converted into sets in the fi
